qa_pairs.question: When did the renewal of the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure begin?
qa_pairs.answer: The Advanced Research Computing infrastructure renewal began in winter 2024–2025.

qa_pairs.question: When were the new Advanced Research Computing systems made available to users?
qa_pairs.answer: New systems were made available to users throughout 2025 and early 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What improvements have been made to High Performance Computing (HPC) and Cloud services for Canadian researchers?
qa_pairs.answer: The changes have improved High Performance Computing (HPC) and Cloud services for Canadian researchers by providing faster processing speeds, greater storage capacity, and improved reliability.

qa_pairs.question: What percentage of the old equipment has been replaced during the infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: The infrastructure renewal has replaced nearly 80% of the equipment that had been approaching end-of-life.

qa_pairs.question: What benefits do the new Advanced Research Computing systems offer?
qa_pairs.answer: The new systems provide faster processing speeds, greater storage capacity, and improved reliability.

qa_pairs.question: Which old system was replaced by Rorqual?
qa_pairs.answer: Rorqual replaced Béluga.

qa_pairs.question: Which old system was replaced by Fir?
qa_pairs.answer: Fir replaced Cedar.

qa_pairs.question: Which old systems were replaced by Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium replaced Niagara and Mist.

qa_pairs.question: Which old system was replaced by Nibi?
qa_pairs.answer: Nibi replaced Graham.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any changes to the Cloud interface with the introduction of Arbutus?
qa_pairs.answer: No, as a virtual infrastructure there is no change to the cloud interface with Arbutus.

qa_pairs.question: Why are outages and reductions unavoidable during the transition to the new infrastructure?
qa_pairs.answer: Outages and reductions will be unavoidable due to constraints on space and electrical power during the installation and transition to the new infrastructure.

qa_pairs.question: What is recommended when planning research programs or graduate examinations during the infrastructure transition?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended that you consider the possibility of outages when you plan research programs, graduate examinations, etc.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find a list of completed infrastructure renewal events?
qa_pairs.answer: For a list of completed events, please see the [[Infrastructure renewal completed events]] page.

qa_pairs.question: What is the status of the Arbutus upgrade and migration?
qa_pairs.answer: The Arbutus upgrade and migration is currently 'In Progress', running from April 7, 2026, to August 31, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Which recipients have access to the upgraded Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: The upgraded Arbutus Cloud is now in production and available to RAC 2026–2027 recipients.

qa_pairs.question: When must RAC recipients and RAS users migrate their existing resources from legacy Arbutus?
qa_pairs.answer: Both RAC recipients and RAS users with a 2025–2026 allocation on legacy Arbutus are responsible for migrating existing resources before August 31, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: When can migration to the new Arbutus Cloud begin?
qa_pairs.answer: Migration may begin as soon as access to the new Arbutus Cloud is available.

qa_pairs.question: Will 2025–2026 RAC allocations remain active on legacy Arbutus during the migration period?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, 2025–2026 RAC allocations will remain active on legacy Arbutus during the migration period.

qa_pairs.question: How do projects with existing Arbutus RAS Cloud allocations get added to the provisioning queue?
qa_pairs.answer: Projects with existing Arbutus RAS Cloud allocations must submit a request to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca to be added to the provisioning queue.

qa_pairs.question: What new authentication method does the new Arbutus Cloud introduce?
qa_pairs.answer: The new Arbutus Cloud introduces single sign-on (SSO) authentication with multi-factor authentication (MFA).

qa_pairs.question: What must users select when logging into the new Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Users must select “Authenticate using Digital Research Alliance of Canada” when logging in.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find documentation for Arbutus migration and multi-factor authentication?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation is available in the Arbutus Migration Guide and Multifactor Authentication guides.

qa_pairs.question: What is the status of the Béluga compute service?
qa_pairs.answer: The Béluga compute service was stopped with the deployment of Rorqual and will not return to service; no restoration or reactivation is planned.

qa_pairs.question: When will storage quotas on Béluga be reduced for data deletion or archiving?
qa_pairs.answer: Storage quotas on `/project`, `/home`, `/scratch`, and `/nearline` will be reduced in January 2026 to allow data deletion or archiving only.

qa_pairs.question: When will the temporary storage system (`/scratch`) on Béluga be decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: The temporary storage system (`/scratch`) will be decommissioned on February 28, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: When will access to all remaining data on Béluga end?
qa_pairs.answer: Access to all remaining data on Béluga will end on June 20, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Is Béluga Cloud impacted by the decommissioning of the Béluga compute cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Béluga Cloud (`beluga-cloud`) is a separate infrastructure and is not impacted by the decommissioning of the Béluga compute cluster.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Niagara compute cluster retired?
qa_pairs.answer: On September 30, 2025, the Niagara compute cluster was retired.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find migration guidance for transitioning from Niagara to Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: For migration guidance, users can see the 'Transition from Niagara to Trillium' page.
